Brainstormed 2 new PENDING features: (1) Keyboard Shortcut Reference — '?' key opens searchable modal of all 25+ shortcuts by category; (2) Handout Print Mode — 2 or 4 slides per page printable grid via window.print() + @media print. Added to deck_roadmap.md Proposed Features.
tick_tock.next_action: Tick — tick_queue_approved is empty. Brainstorm 1 new PENDING YOLO tool idea using boring-but-high-ROI filter. Add to tick_queue_pending. Do NOT build it.

tick_queue_approved.name: semver-range
tick_queue_approved.idea: Semver range calculator — paste a range expression and a list of versions, instantly see which match with pass/fail colors and per-comparison explanation
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Devs constantly need to know whether a package version satisfies a range (^, ~, >=, ||) when debugging package.json dependency conflicts. No offline tool does this cleanly. Implements the full npm semver spec in client-side JS: tilde, caret, comparators, wildcards, union ranges.

tick_queue_approved.name: unix-ts
tick_queue_approved.idea: Unix timestamp debugger — paste any timestamp (auto-detects seconds vs ms), see ISO 8601, UTC, local, RFC 2822, SQL DATETIME, relative time, all with copy buttons; reverse lookup: pick a date and get the epoch
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Backend devs and ops engineers read raw timestamps from logs multiple times a day. No offline tool auto-detects ms vs s, shows all formats simultaneously, and works as a pinned-tab PWA with zero deps. Tight scope, zero dependencies, daily-use utility.

tick_queue_approved.name: uuid-inspector
tick_queue_approved.idea: UUID inspector — paste any UUID, get version detection (v1/v3/v4/v5/v7), decoded timestamp (v1 Gregorian 60-bit + v7 Unix ms), MAC extraction (v1), namespace detection (v3/v5), format validator, bulk mode (one per line), and a generator for all versions
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Backend devs and DBAs encounter UUIDs in every log, DB, and API response. v7 (time-ordered) is now default in Rails 7.1, Laravel 11, and many ORMs so devs urgently need to decode its embedded timestamp. No good offline tool does version-decode + timestamp extraction. Zero external deps, pure JS math, tight scope, ships in one session. Gemini MCP unavailable this session — viability assessed internally.

tick_queue_approved.name: url-dissect
tick_queue_approved.idea: URL Dissector & Analyzer — paste any URL, get instant component breakdown (scheme, userinfo, host, port with protocol-default awareness, path segments decoded, query params as key-value pairs with encoding issues flagged, fragment); plus a relative URL resolver and a live rebuild widget where editing any field reconstructs the full URL; bulk mode extracts all URLs from pasted text
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Backend and frontend devs hit malformed or confusing URLs in logs, OAuth callbacks, webhook configs, and API responses multiple times daily. Browser-native URL() API + URLSearchParams handle all parsing — zero implementation risk, zero deps. No offline tool combines anatomy breakdown + encoding audit + relative resolver in one place. Tight scope, single HTML file, ships in one session. Gemini MCP unavailable — viability assessed internally: HIGH.

tick_queue_approved.name: cron-explain
tick_queue_approved.idea: Cron expression explainer — paste any 5-field or 6-field cron expression, get plain-English description ('At 2:30 AM every Monday'), color-coded field breakdown, next 10 scheduled run times with relative display, and an interactive builder to compose expressions field-by-field from dropdowns with common presets
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Devs write cron jobs constantly (AWS Lambda schedules, GitHub Actions, Kubernetes CronJobs, system cron) but cron syntax is arcane and error-prone. crontab.guru is the canonical reference but is online-only; an offline PWA fills the gap. Pure date-math in JS — zero deps, zero implementation risk. Tight scope, ships in one session. Gemini MCP unavailable — viability assessed internally: HIGH.

tick_queue_approved.name: unicode-char
tick_queue_approved.idea: Unicode Character Inspector — paste any text, get a character-by-character breakdown: codepoint (U+XXXX), official character name, category (Letter/Mark/Separator/etc.), UTF-8 hex bytes, UTF-16 hex, HTML entity, CSS escape, JSON escape; plus a suspicious-character panel flagging zero-width spaces, BOM, invisible separators, and homoglyphs; bulk stats (total chars, bytes, unique codepoints); and a reverse lookup (enter U+XXXX → see the character and all properties)
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Every dev hits 'why is this string comparison failing?' due to invisible characters in logs, API responses, and user input. No offline tool combines anatomy breakdown + encoding formats + security-oriented invisible-char detection in one place. Pure browser JS: codePointAt(), TextEncoder, Intl APIs — zero deps, zero server. Security angle (homoglyph/invisible-char warnings) differentiates it from a plain Unicode table. Tight scope, single HTML file, ships in one session. Gemini MCP unavailable — viability assessed internally: HIGH.

tick_queue_approved.name: ip-cidr
tick_queue_approved.idea: IP/CIDR Calculator & Subnet Analyzer — paste any IPv4 CIDR (e.g. 10.0.1.0/22), get network address, broadcast address, subnet mask, wildcard mask, first/last usable host, total host count, and a binary bitmask visualization; plus an 'Is IP in range?' membership checker and a subnet splitter that divides any parent CIDR into N equal subnets; bulk mode accepts multiple CIDRs for side-by-side comparison
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Cloud/backend/devops engineers deal with CIDR blocks every day — AWS/GCP/Azure VPC subnets, Kubernetes pod and service CIDRs, firewall ingress rules, security groups, network ACLs. The mental math is error-prone (especially non-power-of-2 splits). No offline tool combines analysis + membership check + subnet splitting in one place. Pure 32-bit bitwise integer math in JS — zero external deps, zero server. On-call engineers need this on mobile; PWA makes it a pinned tool. Scope fits one session cleanly. Gemini MCP unavailable — viability assessed internally: HIGH.

tick_queue_approved.name: port-ref
tick_queue_approved.idea: Well-Known Port Quick-Reference — type a port number or service name to instantly see the official IANA service, protocol (TCP/UDP/both), privilege boundary warning (<1024 requires root on Linux), and a 'commonly scanned/attacked?' security flag; reverse-lookup by service name; bulk mode: paste a block of port numbers (from docker-compose, k8s manifests, or firewall rules) and get an annotated table with service names and risk flags; offline PWA
tick_queue_approved.rationale: Devops, backend, and cloud engineers consult port numbers constantly — docker-compose ports:, k8s service manifests, AWS security group inbound rules, Dockerfile EXPOSE, firewall ACLs. 'What uses port 9092?' (Kafka), 'what port is Redis?' are Googled dozens of times per week. No offline tool combines: port→service lookup + service→port reverse search + privilege boundary (<1024) + security risk flagging + bulk annotation from pasted config snippets. Pure JS static lookup table (~300 well-known ports from IANA) — zero external deps, zero server. PWA pinned tab for on-call engineers. Tight scope, ships in one session. Gemini MCP unavailable — viability assessed internally: HIGH.
